heading: Requirement for authorization of appropriations
label: 6212 Requirement for authorization of appropriations
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in subsec. (c)(2), was in the original “this title”, meaning title III of , , , known as the United States International Broadcasting Act of 1994, which is classified principally to this chapter. Amendments2021—Subsec. (a). substituted “Agency” for “Board” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 116–2832002—Subsec. (a). struck out “the direction and” after “broadcasting subject to” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 107–228

Continued Availability of Funds Following Repeal of Board for International Broadcasting Act of 1973, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 103–317, title V108 Stat. 1771Aug. 26, 1994“That on the date upon which the Board for International Broadcasting Act of 1973 (, et seq.) is repealed, as provided for by section 310(e) of the Foreign Relations Authorization Act, fiscal years 1994 and 1995 (; ) [former ], funds made available for expenses of the Board for International Broadcasting shall be made available until expended only for expenses necessary to enable the Broadcasting Board of Governors [now United States Agency for Global Media] to carry out the authorities provided in [], including the appointment of staff personnel as authorized by : , That such amounts appropriated to the Board for International Broadcasting in fiscal year 1994 as are certified by the Office of Management and Budget to the Congress as gains due to the fluctuation of foreign currency, may be used in fiscal year 1995 and thereafter either to offset foreign currency losses or to offset unfunded RFE/RL costs associated with the implementation of [see Tables for classification]: , That obligated but unexpended balances appropriated in fiscal year 1990 to fund planned transmitter modernization expenses may be expended in fiscal year 1995 for unfunded RFE/RL costs associated with the implementation of ”.22 U.S.C. 2871Public Law 103–236108 Stat. 44222 U.S.C. 6209(e)section 305(a) of Public Law 103–23622 U.S.C. 6204(a)section 305(a)(11) of Public Law 103–236Public Law 103–236Public Law 103–236Provided furtherProvided further

children.heading: Limitation on obligation and expenditure of funds
children.label: a Limitation on obligation and expenditure of funds

children.text: unless such funds are appropriated pursuant to an authorization of appropriations; or
in excess of the authorized level of appropriations.

children.heading: Subsequent authorization
children.label: b Subsequent authorization
children.text: The limitation under subsection (a) shall not apply to the extent that an authorization of appropriations is enacted after such funds are appropriated.
may not be superseded, except by a provision of law which specifically repeals, modifies, or supersedes the provisions of this section; and
shall not apply to, or affect in any manner, permanent appropriations, trust funds, and other similar accounts which are authorized by law and administered under or pursuant to this chapter.
